meaning: The flow could not convert a value into the data type expected by the next action. This article focuses on the approval workflow scenario.
causes: String, number, boolean, object, array, date, or binary content is passed to an incompatible input. In this scenario, check approver identity, approval timeout, response schema, action run-after settings, and notification delivery.
resolution: Compare the action input to the connector schema, normalize values with explicit conversion functions, and retry. For approval workflow, also verify: Check approver identity, approval timeout, response schema, action run-after settings, and notification delivery.
prevention: Validate connector-bound payloads and keep example payloads for regression testing. For approval workflow, document the expected permissions, connector settings, and test data used to validate the fix.

prevention: Monitor credential expiry and avoid personal-user connections for critical production automations. For copilot studio topic, document the expected permissions, connector settings, and test data used to validate the fix.

causes: Webhook callback never arrived, approval has no expiration, delay exceeds run duration, or external service stayed unavailable. In this scenario, check environment URLs, connection references, callback URLs, app registrations, data source IDs, and tenant policies.
resolution: Set explicit timeouts, configure run-after timeout branches, and redesign long waits into relay-style runs. For environment move or copy, also verify: Check environment URLs, connection references, callback URLs, app registrations, data source IDs, and tenant policies.
prevention: Avoid unbounded waits in production flows. For environment move or copy, document the expected permissions, connector settings, and test data used to validate the fix.

meaning: Dataverse service protection limits are being triggered by excessive API demand. This article focuses on the on-premises gateway data source scenario.
causes: Too many requests, too much execution time, high concurrency, aggressive retries, or unbatched high-volume workload. In this scenario, check gateway online status, data source mapping, credentials, network reachability, and connector permissions.
resolution: Honor Retry-After, reduce concurrency, cache reads, batch carefully, and spread work over time. For on-premises gateway data source, also verify: Check gateway online status, data source mapping, credentials, network reachability, and connector permissions.
prevention: Implement backoff and monitor request volume for integrations. For on-premises gateway data source, document the expected permissions, connector settings, and test data used to validate the fix.

seoTitle: Too Many Requests in On-premises Gateway Data Source | Power Platform Error Fix
metaDescription: Too Many Requests in On-premises Gateway Data Source: what it means, common causes, and how to resolve it in Dataverse.

appliesTo: Power Platform environment copy, restore, backup, or tenant move
scenario: An environment is copied, restored, moved, or reconfigured and existing apps or flows are tested afterward.
meaning: Too many operations are running concurrently for the connector, environment, user, or service. This article focuses on the environment move or copy scenario.
causes: High parallelism, multiple flows sharing the same connection, nested loops, or aggressive retry settings. In this scenario, check environment URLs, connection references, callback URLs, app registrations, data source IDs, and tenant policies.
resolution: Reduce degree of parallelism, stagger schedules, use queues, and tune retry policies. For environment move or copy, also verify: Check environment URLs, connection references, callback URLs, app registrations, data source IDs, and tenant policies.
prevention: Set concurrency limits deliberately for high-volume automations. For environment move or copy, document the expected permissions, connector settings, and test data used to validate the fix.

appliesTo: Power Platform connection using app registration or service principal authentication
scenario: A flow, app, or integration authenticates with an application identity instead of a personal user account.
meaning: The Excel connector cannot find the row matching the supplied key value. This article focuses on the service principal connection scenario.
causes: Wrong key column, blank key value, duplicate keys, row deleted, or workbook not refreshed. In this scenario, check app registration permissions, consent, secret expiry, certificate validity, security roles, and connection ownership.
